Narrative_Meaning: Eights signify karma and mostly in the form of karmic retributions, if someone has been having a bad time now is the time they will be rewarded, if they have been running amok they will now have to pay that price.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Scythe card represents an honest day's work, usually manual labour, often field or yard work (throughout human history) in a hot harvest sun, but it can represent any work with tools, labour, gathering or harvest activities. The Scythe can also represent danger at work, and of course has a long association with both Death and Time.

Significator.Plot: The Scythe Plot is about everyday life and death. Death is a hum-drum event, it is constantly occurring all around us, this Plot shows us that, but often not through violence or pestilence. The Scythe is a Plot of small, everyday dramas, soap opera, ordinary lives stuff, but death is always a part of that Tapestry. Old people, the ill, the careless, the unlucky, the Scythe may take a swing at them all, and keeps swinging until someone dies.
Significator.Side: When a side is marked with the Scythe then one of the Embodiments is slated to die. It may be the event that kicks off the Conflict as an inciting event, or it may occur in the Zenith, but at least one Embodiment on that side must die.
Significator.Character:
The Scythe Significator indicates a Character that works hard and expects rewards.

They Gain Yin when they complain, grumble, or moan.

They Gain Yang when they do work.

They Gain Chi when they rest, or are paid after work.

They gain +1 Success Levels to any task when they complain about it.
Significator.Scene: The Scythe Scene is an Ordeal or Test usually, that features hard work, or work-related injuries, although it can be a Gain in terms of a payment or reward that has been earned. Yarn-Tellers can play the card to injure or just make things harder for a target, or to perform an arduous task automatically such as gathering food, or as a payment such as a salary.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Skills indicating that only someone with those precise skills can truly solve this problem. The opposition may even be targeting people known to possess certain Skills.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Story opens with a call back to something that happened in a previous Story. This story could be a spin-off from the original, or this could just be a reuse of a location, descendant or character.
Yarn.Fray_Description: Hard Work and Labour is usually an Extended Ordeal, there is not often opposition, but there may be a requirement for multiple people to work together, or at a pace. The Stakes are usually Low or Medium, but higher Stakes Labour exist (miners, lumberjacks and crab fisherman spring to mind as high or extreme Stakes labour). The point is this work is hard, sweaty, exhausting and will push the PCs to exhaustion.
Yarn.Snag_Description: When Peasants revolt they have a tendency to grab anything that they can use as a weapon, considering their day to day life involves mowing grass with scythes, lifting the mowed grass with pitchforks and so on they get quite well trained with these tools. This Snag means that whomever is involved in the Fray they are more heavily armed (and better equipped and trained) than you might have imagined.

NPC.Description: The Farmhand is a simple labourer who happens to be an expert with their preferred tool, whether it be a scythe, a rope, a tractor, or moisture-vaporator

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PCs will make a major Gain (such as a harvest) if they succeed in a Test or Ordeal (such as Harvesting).
Yarn.History_Description: The Scythe indicates that history leading to this current situation has been worked for by someone. This may mean it was someone's full-time job, or just that lots of people helped some mastermind.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Scythe indicates that the current situation is dangerous, often this is an occupational hazard, some sort of danger at work, which may be accidental or deliberate. Often this danger will be great enough to raise the Stakes to High or more.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Scythe indicates that the solution to the current situation is a specialised tool that must be acquired, created, invented, purchased, stolen, summoned or found somehow.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about one  side of the Conflict, or all. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.
Revelations.Info_Description: Information that tells us something about amounts, volumes, or weights. It could be as simple as a receipt, or a tax return, or as complex as the number of work-hours something might take, but often reveals something more interesting than you'd think.
Revelations.Vector_Description: Wounds can carry information, even if it is the wounded messenger that carries them. Even the wounds on a corpse can tell us things about the height, handedness, strength and weight of the attacker, as well as information about the weapon used if you know how to read them. Especially if the bad guy used them to leave letters, perhaps spelling "DETH", such a message might be a damning indictment of the education system, or point to a specific group or killer.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is about the work of the Embodiment, whether that is work that they have done, are currently doing or will do. This is not necessarily the profession of the Embodiment, but more their accomplishments, deeds, or achievements.
Revelations.Detail_Description: Morbid details are details of an especially gloomy, grisly, and gruesome nature, they tend to focus on darkness, depression, death and disease, aggrandizing and even glorifying these, although in a chilling and cheerless way. These may be details about the Embodiment themselves, detailing their long fight with cancer, the details of the afternoon and evening when their family were butchered, or about the Embodiments own "achievements" in these realms, such as a serial killer's kill count.

Obstacle.Description: The Obstacle requires work to be done to pass it. This might be pulling a rope, turning a wheel, drawing water, shovelling coal, splitting wood, that primes a mechanism, lowers a bridge, or opens a way.

Tarot.Normal: Add a card from your Hand to the Target's Hand and Draw 2 cards.
Tarot.Crossed: Take a random card from the Target's Hand and add it to your own. They may draw a replacement.

Ordeal.Stage_Description: The way ahead is some sort of webbing, net, or weave, maybe the internet, or perhaps a suspension bridge, or maybe a confluence of roads, threads or wires. Or perhaps the way ahead is just heavy with old cobwebs.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: They're just ahead, you can hear the pounding hoof-beats on the dark forest road. You pursue pushing your horse harder, until you can see their cloaks flapping in the wind. One turns in the saddle, there is a flash of fire that reveals his face, masked beneath his Tricorn hat, and then the crack-whistle of a musket shot.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: Several members of the Ebbing side are taken prisoner or surrender. The Flowing side must decide to capture and guard the prisoners, or whether to take no prisoners, either way may have positive or negative effects on both sides, depending how things unfold.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
The enemy needs to be captured alive (and conscious ideally). Usually this enemy is a solitary Monster (Cast Extra) with 2 Monster Facets .
  Facet    Monster  
Burden ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.

Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

Parry:DisarmAll the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Object — The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Boon. Objects are able increase the Scores of any rolled die and Pips of all played cards by their Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may add Burden Score cards to it’s Monster Pool, if it makes, or has made, modifications, or upgrades to itself, usually using components it has secured. It may add Burden Draw cards to its Monster Pool any time it is mistaken for a normal Object, or is used in accordance to its form or function. The Object Monster must play cards from its Monster Pool to perform any action that is not a part of its normal form and function.
Dominion ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Horde — The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. A Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their individual Values. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in number. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that potentially splits or reduces the Horde (like losing an individual or being attacked). If the Horde Monster Pool is emptied the Horde is scattered as individuals again, and must reform to regain Monster Pool. The Horde automatically adds the current Monster Pool Size (Number of cards) to pay any costs for multiple targets and may also add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Pips to any perception-based Actions. E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1-3 cards in the Monster Pool granting them a bonus to offset any costs from multiple targets and a smaller bonus to perception) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual’s Value(45). If there were 12, or One million, Vexes would give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.
Fury ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Temperamental — The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as undying and shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is embodiment of berserk rage, and is almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience or induce their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When affecting a Character who is afflicted with their emotion they may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to their Actions. Temperamentals that are affected by emotional attacks must use cards from their Monster Pool to Ignore (Drawing equal to their Number of Active Hitches Reduced) the effect.
Rook ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Guardian — The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardian Monsters may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they interfere with another’s attack, or are attacked themselves. The Guardian will add Rook Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever they, or the thing they guard is Wounded or Damaged. A Guardian may spend a card from its Monster Pool to cancel another’s attack, this can be described as a dodge, or a soak. A Guardian may add a Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to any Attack or Defence action. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory. Guardians often use area-denial Splash attacks, such as breath weapons, tail lashes, wing buffeted dust, venom sprays, gaze attacks, and explosions, to drive enemies from their territory.
Sin ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Villain — The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (usually with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another, indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as “Evil” or as focused as “Smokes Gauloises Cigarettes” as you like. Villains may add +1 Score / Pips for each card in their Monster Pool to any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Sin Draw Monster Pool cards on creating Obstacles when they escape or are threatened. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.
Wyrd ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Doom-weaver — The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver Monster may draw and Prepare Monster Pool Size Reduced cards whenever anyone else plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards in the same Location. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, or even just powerful Mercari, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Notes: These are the most commonly walked paths amongst the Herd. The Paths often blend numerous animals together into super-herds that are common in Africa.

Stress.Description: Characters deep in Stress can struggle to cope. It can feel like they are drowning, unable to catch a breath between the wild buffets of fate. If they were less stressed they would cope, but it seems like everything is more difficult for these Characters now. Somehow though they will often struggle through, barely making ends meet until the stress lifts.
Stress.Rules: Club Suited Facet Actions (Burden, Dominion, Fury, Rook, Sin, or Wyrd) add Difficulty equal to the current Stress in Chi, other Facet Actions add Difficulty equal to the current Stress as Tao Sway. If a penalised Action is a Stalemate then the Character may reduce Stress, Strain and Shock by 1.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can drive a Character to focus on their work as a coping mechanism. Some people find work a lot less anxiety inducing than the rest of their life, and for them it becomes an easy decision to invest more of their time into their work.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma makes a Character reprioritize their life, such that they can now Relieve 1 Stress by focusing on their work over everything else.

Age.Description: An Age of Accumulation is occurring. During this age a society reaps the rewards of their hard work and planning. This is a time of production, resource gathering, and manufacturing success and generates wealth for the society, individual members get richer, financially and culturally and society improves as a result. The age cultivates new cultural, artistic, manufacturing, management, and educational trends within the society, bringing in new knowledge, resources, techniques, styles and sciences from other societies, which benefit the society greatly moving forwards. This accumulation of knowledge, wealth and power leads to firther innovations and gains that can be made before the age ends.
Age.Rules: During an Age of Accumulation a society and the individual members make Gains, often of wealth, resources, technologies, knowledge and fashions that lead to further accumulations.
